curriculum.subject: Physics
curriculum.gradeLevel: Class XI / Class XII / 12th Grade / Science
curriculum.overallDescription: 19 units, 28 chapters, and 5 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand the need for measurement, systems of units, SI units (fundamental and derived), significant figures, uncertainty determination, dimensions of physical quantities, and dimensional analysis.

Apply elementary concepts of differentiation and integration to describe motion, distinguish between uniform/non-uniform motion, calculate average/instantaneous velocity, and use relations for uniformly accelerated motion (graphical/calculus).

Master general vectors, notations, equality, multiplication by real numbers, addition/subtraction of vectors, unit vectors, resolution into rectangular components, scalar/vector products, projectile motion, uniform circular motion.
Understand Newton's laws (1st, 2nd with momentum/impulse, 3rd), conservation of linear momentum, equilibrium of concurrent forces, friction types, and dynamics of uniform circular motion.
Apply the work-energy theorem, understand power, potential energy (including springs), conservative/non-conservative forces, motion in vertical circles, and elastic/inelastic collisions.

Analyze momentum conservation, centre of mass motion, torque, angular momentum, equilibrium of rigid bodies, rotational equations, comparison with linear motion, moment of inertia, and radius of gyration.
Apply the universal law of gravitation, understand variation in acceleration due to gravity, gravitational potential energy/potential, escape speed, orbital velocity, and satellite energy.
Understand Hooke's law, Young’s modulus, bulk/shear moduli (qualitative), Poisson's ratio, elastic energy, and applications of elastic behavior.

Analyze viscosity, Stokes' law, terminal velocity, streamline/turbulent flow, Bernoulli's theorem (Torricelli/dynamic lift), surface tension/energy, angle of contact, and excess pressure applications.
Understand specific heat capacity, calorimetry, latent heat, modes of heat transfer (conduction/convection/radiation), thermal conductivity, and blackbody radiation concepts.
Distinguish heat, work, internal energy; apply First and Second laws, understand state variables/equations of state, isothermal/adiabatic processes, reversible/irreversible/cyclic processes.
Apply kinetic theory assumptions, understand pressure concept, temperature interpretation (RMS speed), degrees of freedom, equipartition law, specific heats, mean free path, and Avogadro's number.

practicals.title: To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body and to measure internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using Vernier Callipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of a given wire and thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine volume of an irregular lamina using screw gauge.
To determine radius of curvature of a given spherical surface by a spherometer.
To determine the mass of two different objects using a beam balance.
To find the weight of a given body using parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum, plot its graph and use it to find the effective length of second's pendulum.
To study variation of time period of a simple pendulum of a given length by taking bobs of same size but different masses and interpret the result.
To study the relationship between force of limiting friction and normal reaction and to find the co- efficient of friction between a block and a horizontal surface.

To find the downward force, along an inclined plane, acting on a roller due to gravitational pull of the earth and study its relationship with the angle of inclination θ by plotting graph between force and Sinθ.
To determine Young's modulus of elasticity of the material of a given wire.
To find the force constant of a helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
To study the variation in volume with pressure for a sample of air at constant temperature by plotting graphs between P and V, and between P and 1/V.
To determine the surface tension of water by capillary rise method.
To determine the coefficient of viscosity of a given viscous liquid by measuring terminal velocity of a given spherical body.
To study the relationship between the temperature of a hot body and time by plotting a cooling curve.
To determine specific heat capacity of a given solid by method of mixtures.
To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using sonometer.
To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension for constant frequency using sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air at room temperature using a resonance tube by two resonance positions.
To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current.
To find resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using metre bridge.
To verify the laws of combination (series) of resistances using a metre bridge OR To verify the laws of combination (parallel) of resistances using a metre bridge.
To determine resistance of a galvanometer by half-deflection method and to find its figure of merit.

To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into a voltmeter of desired range and to verify the same OR To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into an ammeter of desired range and to verify the same.
To find the frequency of AC mains with a sonometer.
To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current.
To measure the internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using vernier calipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of given wire using screw gauge.
To measure thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine the mass of a given object using a beam balance.
To find the weight of given body using the parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum plot L-T and graphs. Hence find the effective length of second's pendulum using appropriate length values.
To find the force constant of given helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using a sonometer.
To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension, for constant frequency, using a sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air, at room temperature, using a resonance tube, by observing the two resonance positions.

competency_groups.title: Physical World and Measurement - Units and Measurements
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to measurement systems, SI units, significant figures, uncertainty analysis, dimensional analysis, and their applications.
competencies: Understand the need for measurement and various systems of units including SI units (fundamental and derived).
Apply rules for significant figures and determine uncertainty in results.
Perform dimensional analysis to check consistency of physical equations and derive relationships between quantities.

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Straight Line
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to describing motion using calculus, understanding different types of motion, interpreting graphs, and applying equations for uniformly accelerated motion.
competencies: Apply elementary concepts of differentiation and integration to describe motion.
Distinguish between uniform and non-uniform motion and calculate average speed/velocity versus instantaneous velocity.
Interpret position-time and velocity-time graphs.
Derive and apply relations for uniformly accelerated motion using graphical methods and calculus.

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Plane
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to vector algebra, resolution of vectors, and analyzing motion in two dimensions including projectile and circular motion.
competencies: Perform operations on scalar and vector quantities (addition, subtraction, multiplication by real number).
Resolve vectors into rectangular components and calculate scalar/vector products.
Analyze uniform velocity/acceleration, projectile motion, and uniform circular motion in a plane.

competency_groups.title: Laws of Motion - Dynamics and Equilibrium
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Newton's laws, momentum conservation, impulse, friction, and equilibrium conditions.
competencies: Explain inertia and apply Newton's first law (Law of Inertia).
Apply Newton's second law relating force, mass, acceleration, and change in momentum; understand Impulse.
Apply Newton's third law to interactions between bodies.
Use the principle of conservation of linear momentum for collision problems.
Analyze equilibrium conditions involving static/kinetic friction, rolling friction, and lubrication.

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy and Power - Conservation Laws
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to work-energy theorem, power, potential energy types, conservative forces, collisions, and motion in vertical circles.
competencies: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces and apply the Work-Energy Theorem.
Distinguish between kinetic and potential energies (including spring) and identify conservative vs. non-conservative forces.
Analyze elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions.
Apply energy conservation principles to motion in a vertical circle.

competency_groups.title: Motion of System of Particles - Rotational Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to center of mass, torque, angular momentum conservation, rotational motion equations, moment of inertia, and comparison with linear motion.
competencies: Determine the center of mass for two-particle systems and rigid bodies (e.g., uniform rod).
Apply laws of conservation of momentum and angular momentum to physical scenarios.
Analyze equilibrium conditions for rigid bodies under concurrent forces.
Derive and apply equations of rotational motion; compare linear and rotational kinematics/dynamics.
Calculate moment of inertia and radius of gyration for simple geometrical objects.
competency_groups.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation - Universal Law and Satellite Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Kepler's laws, universal gravitation, variation in gravity, gravitational potential/energy, orbital mechanics, and escape speed.
competencies: State and apply Kepler's laws of planetary motion.
Apply the Universal Law of Gravitation to calculate forces between masses.
Analyze variations in acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth.
Calculate gravitational potential energy and potential; determine escape speed and orbital velocity for satellites.

competency_groups.title: Properties of Bulk Matter - Solids, Fluids, and Thermal Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to elasticity (stress-strain), fluid mechanics (viscosity, Bernoulli's theorem), surface tension, heat transfer mechanisms, and blackbody radiation.
competencies: Apply Hooke's Law and calculate Young’s modulus, Bulk modulus, Shear modulus, Poisson's ratio; understand elastic energy.
Analyze fluid properties: viscosity (Stokes' law, terminal velocity), streamline/turbulent flow, Bernoulli's theorem, surface tension, capillary action.
Differentiate between heat and temperature; calculate specific heats and latent heat of fusion/vaporization.
Explain mechanisms of heat transfer (conduction, convection, radiation) and apply concepts of blackbody radiation (Wien’s law, Stefan’s law).
competency_groups.chapter_name: Mechanical Properties of Solids; Mechanical Properties of Fluids; Thermal Properties of Matter

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics - Laws and Processes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to thermal equilibrium, laws of thermodynamics (zeroth through second), state variables, equations of state, and various thermodynamic processes.
competencies: Define temperature using the Zeroth Law; distinguish between heat, work, and internal energy.
Apply the First Law of Thermodynamics to calculate changes in internal energy.
Understand the Second Law regarding reversible/irreversible processes and cyclic processes.
Analyze isothermal, adiabatic, and other gaseous state conditions using equations of state.

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory - Gas Behavior and Molecular Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to kinetic theory assumptions, pressure derivation, temperature interpretation, degrees of freedom, specific heats, mean free path, and Avogadro's number.
competencies: Derive the concept of gas pressure from kinetic theory assumptions.
Interpret temperature as a measure of average kinetic energy; calculate RMS speed.
Apply the law of equipartition of energy to specific heat capacities of gases.
Understand concepts of mean free path and Avogadro's number.
competency_groups.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Oscillations - Simple Harmonic Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to periodic motion, SHM equations, energy in oscillation, and simple pendulum dynamics.
competencies: Distinguish between general periodic functions and Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM).
Derive and apply the equation of motion for a loaded spring and a simple pendulum.
Analyze kinetic and potential energy variations in SHM.

competency_groups.title: Waves - Wave Motion and Superposition
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to wave types, propagation speed, superposition principle, standing waves, beats, reflection, and interference.
competencies: Differentiate between transverse and longitudinal waves; calculate wave speed.
Apply the principle of superposition to analyze reflections and formation of standing waves in strings/pipes (fundamental mode/harmonics).
Explain the phenomenon of beats.

competency_groups.title: Electrostatics - Charges, Fields, and Potentials
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to conservation of charge, Coulomb's law, superposition principle, electric fields (point/dipole), Gauss's theorem applications, potential energy, dielectrics, and capacitance.
competencies: Apply the principle of conservation of charge and Coulomb's Law for multiple charges using superposition.
Calculate electric field due to point charges, dipoles, continuous distributions; determine torque on a dipole in an external field.
Use Gauss's theorem to derive fields for infinite wire, plane sheet, and spherical shell.
Analyze equipotential surfaces, potential energy of charge systems, dielectric polarization, and capacitor combinations (series/parallel).
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–1: Electric Charges and Fields; Chapter–2: Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance

competency_groups.title: Current Electricity - Drift, Ohm's Law, and Circuits
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to drift velocity, mobility, Ohm's law characteristics, resistivity/conductivity, internal resistance of cells, Kirchhoff's rules, and Wheatstone bridge.
competencies: Relate electric current to drift velocity and mobility; analyze V-I characteristics (linear/non-linear).
Apply concepts of electrical energy/power, resistivity, conductivity, and temperature dependence of resistance.
Analyze internal resistance of cells, EMF vs. potential difference, and combinations of cells.
Use Kirchhoff's rules to solve complex circuits; apply the Wheatstone bridge principle.

competency_groups.title: Magnetism - Fields, Forces, and Magnetic Materials
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to magnetic field concepts (Biot-Savart, Ampere's law), forces on charges/conductors, torque on loops, galvanometer conversion, and properties of magnetic materials.
competencies: Apply Biot-Savart Law and Ampere's Law to calculate fields from current-carrying conductors (loops, wires).
Calculate force on moving charges/currents in magnetic fields; analyze torque on current loops and magnetic dipoles.
Understand the conversion of galvanometers into ammeters/voltmeters.
Classify materials as para-, dia-, or ferro-magnetic; explain qualitative behavior of bar magnets.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–4: Moving Charges and Magnetism; Chapter–5: Magnetism and Matter

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction - Laws and Applications
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Faraday's laws, Lenz's law, self/mutual induction, AC circuits (reactance/impedance), resonance, power factor, generators, and transformers.
competencies: Apply Faraday's Law of Induction and Lenz's Law to determine induced EMF/current direction.
Understand concepts of self-inductance and mutual induction.
Analyze AC circuits: peak/RMS values, reactance/impedance in RLC series circuits (phasors), resonance conditions, power factor, wattless current.
Explain the working principles of AC generators and transformers.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–6: Electromagnetic Induction; Chapter–7: Alternating Current

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves - Spectrum and Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to displacement current, nature of EM waves (transverse), characteristics, and the electromagnetic spectrum.
competencies: Understand the concept of displacement current leading to the existence of electromagnetic waves.
Describe the transverse nature and basic properties of EM waves.
Identify regions of the electromagnetic spectrum (radio to gamma rays) and their elementary applications.

competency_groups.title: Optics - Ray and Wave Optics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to reflection/refraction laws, mirrors/lenses formulas, optical instruments (microscopes/telescopes), Huygens' principle, interference, diffraction.
competencies: Apply mirror formula, lens maker's formula, and magnification concepts for spherical surfaces and thin lenses; analyze combinations of lenses/prisms.
Explain total internal reflection and its applications (optical fibers).
Describe optical instruments: microscopes and telescopes (reflecting/refracting) and their magnifying powers.
Apply Huygens' principle to derive laws of reflection/refraction; analyze interference (Young's double slit), diffraction, and coherence.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments; Chapter–10: Wave Optics

competency_groups.title: Dual Nature of Radiation - Photoelectric Effect and Matter Waves
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to photoelectric effect observations, Einstein's equation, particle nature of light, de-Broglie relation, and matter waves.
competencies: Explain the experimental study of the photoelectric effect (Hertz/Lenard) and its implications for the particle nature of light.
Apply Einstein's photoelectric equation to calculate kinetic energy of emitted electrons.
Understand de-Broglie relation connecting momentum and wavelength; explain matter waves.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

competency_groups.title: Atoms and Nuclei - Models, Spectra, and Nuclear Physics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Rutherford/Bohr models, hydrogen spectra, nuclear composition, binding energy, fission/fusion.
competencies: Explain the alpha-particle scattering experiment leading to the nuclear model of the atom; describe Bohr's model and derive expressions for orbit radius/velocity/energy.
Analyze hydrogen line spectra (qualitative).
Describe nuclear composition, size, mass-energy relation, binding energy per nucleon curve.
Explain processes of nuclear fission and fusion.

competency_groups.title: Electronic Devices - Semiconductors and Diodes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to energy bands, intrinsic/extrinsic semiconductors, p-n junctions, diode characteristics (forward/reverse bias), rectification.
competencies: Distinguish between conductors, insulators, and semiconductors based on energy band theory.
Differentiate between n-type and p-type extrinsic semiconductors; explain formation of the p-n junction.
Analyze I-V characteristics of a diode in forward and reverse bias; understand its application as a rectifier.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits

learning_outcomes.topic: Need for measurement; Units of measurement; Systems of units
outcomes: Understand the need for measurement, systems of units, SI units (fundamental and derived), significant figures, uncertainty determination, dimensions of physical quantities, and dimensional analysis.

learning_outcomes.topic: Frame of reference; Motion in a straight line
outcomes: Apply elementary concepts of differentiation and integration to describe motion, distinguish between uniform/non-uniform motion, calculate average/instantaneous velocity, and use relations for uniformly accelerated motion (graphical/calculus).

learning_outcomes.topic: Scalar and vector quantities; Position and displacement vectors
outcomes: Master general vectors, notations, equality, multiplication by real numbers, addition/subtraction of vectors, unit vectors, resolution into rectangular components, scalar/vector products, projectile motion, uniform circular motion.
Understand Newton's laws (1st, 2nd with momentum/impulse, 3rd), conservation of linear momentum, equilibrium of concurrent forces, friction types, and dynamics of uniform circular motion.

learning_outcomes.topic: Work done by constant/variable force; Kinetic energy
outcomes: Apply the work-energy theorem, understand power, potential energy (including springs), conservative/non-conservative forces, motion in vertical circles, and elastic/inelastic collisions.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
learning_outcomes.topic: Centre of mass: Two-particle system, Rigid body (uniform rod)
outcomes: Analyze momentum conservation, centre of mass motion, torque, angular momentum, equilibrium of rigid bodies, rotational equations, comparison with linear motion, moment of inertia, and radius of gyration.
Apply the universal law of gravitation, understand variation in acceleration due to gravity, gravitational potential energy/potential, escape speed, orbital velocity, and satellite energy.
Understand Hooke's law, Young’s modulus, bulk/shear moduli (qualitative), Poisson's ratio, elastic energy, and applications of elastic behavior.

learning_outcomes.topic: Pressure due to fluid column; Pascal's law
outcomes: Analyze viscosity, Stokes' law, terminal velocity, streamline/turbulent flow, Bernoulli's theorem (Torricelli/dynamic lift), surface tension/energy, angle of contact, and excess pressure applications.

learning_outcomes.topic: Heat, Temperature; Thermal expansion (solids, liquids, gases)
outcomes: Understand specific heat capacity, calorimetry, latent heat, modes of heat transfer (conduction/convection/radiation), thermal conductivity, and blackbody radiation concepts.

learning_outcomes.topic: Thermal equilibrium; Zeroth law of thermodynamics
outcomes: Distinguish heat, work, internal energy; apply First and Second laws, understand state variables/equations of state, isothermal/adiabatic processes, reversible/irreversible/cyclic processes.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases
learning_outcomes.topic: Equation of state; Work done in compressing gas
outcomes: Apply kinetic theory assumptions, understand pressure concept, temperature interpretation (RMS speed), degrees of freedom, equipartition law, specific heats, mean free path, and Avogadro's number.
Describe periodic functions, Simple Harmonic Motion (S.H.M), uniform circular motion relations, phase, loaded spring oscillations, energy in S.H.M., and simple pendulum time period.

learning_outcomes.topic: Wave motion: Transverse and longitudinal waves
outcomes: Analyze speed of travelling waves, displacement relations, superposition principle, reflection, standing waves (strings/pipes), harmonics, and beats.

learning_outcomes.topic: Electric charges and Conservation of charge
outcomes: Apply Coulomb's law, superposition principle for multiple/continuous charges, understand electric fields (point/dipole), torque on dipoles, Gauss's theorem applications.

learning_outcomes.topic: Electric potential, potential difference, equipotential surfaces
outcomes: Calculate electric potential/energy for various charge distributions, understand conductors/insulators/dielectrics/polarization, capacitance concepts, series/parallel combinations.
Understand Ohm's law, resistivity/conductivity/temperature dependence, internal resistance/emf, cell combinations, Kirchhoff's rules, Wheatstone bridge.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
outcomes: Apply Biot-Savart/Ampere's laws, calculate forces on charges/conductors, understand torque on loops, magnetic dipole moment, moving coil galvanometer conversions.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
outcomes: Understand bar magnets as solenoids, magnetic field intensity (qualitative), torque on dipoles, classification of magnetic materials.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
outcomes: Apply Faraday's laws, Lenz's law, understand self/mutual induction.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
outcomes: Analyze peak/RMS values, reactance/impedance in LCR circuits (phasors), resonance, power factor/wattless current, AC generators and transformers.
Understand displacement current, characteristics/transverse nature of EM waves, and the electromagnetic spectrum.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
outcomes: Apply laws of reflection/refraction, mirror/lens formulas, total internal reflection (optical fibers), prism refraction, magnification/power, optical instruments.
Apply Huygen's principle to reflection/refraction, understand interference (Young's experiment), diffraction (single slit).

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
outcomes: Understand photoelectric effect (Einstein's equation), particle nature of light, matter waves (de-Broglie relation).
Understand Rutherford's model, Bohr model (radius/velocity/energy), hydrogen line spectra.
Understand nuclear composition/size, mass-energy relation, binding energy per nucleon, fission/fusion.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
outcomes: Understand energy bands (conductors/semiconductors), intrinsic/extrinsic semiconductors, p-n junction diode characteristics, rectification.
